Motion 1. For Issuance Of A Letter Rogatory To The High Court Of Hong Kong [Unopposed]; And 2. For An Order Modifying Protective Order To Permit Submission To Hong Kong Court [Opposed] **Redacted**
Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kirby Hsu, Esq. 213-955-9240() for Plaintiff.
Timothy Miller, Esq. 650-966-5113 Valled Makoff LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. Defendant's counsel Mr. Miller contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments. Mr. Hsu requests the tentative ruling be adopted. The Court states parties to work out the protective order.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HP INC. Motion 1. For Issuance Of A Letter Rogatory To The High Court Of Hong Kong [Unopposed]; And 2. For An Order Modifying Protective Order To Permit Submission To Hong Kong Court [Opposed] **Redacted**

Defendant HP Inc.'s Motion for Issuance of a Letter Rogatory and for an Order Modifying Protective Order to Permit Submission to Hong Kong Court is GRATNED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Defendant has established that a letter rogatory is necessary and convenient. (See Code Civil Procedure section 2027.010(e).) Good cause appearing, the requested letter rogatory shall issue.

Defendant has not established good cause to support an order modifying the protective order. The Hong Kong court may expect "background documents" and such documents may be "standard." But it is not clear that background documents are necessary necessary. Indeed, this court has already ruled on the need for the discovery. Even if some background documents are necessary, Defendant's request is wildly overbroad and is denied on this basis. The court will not cull the 20 or more exhibits to determine what is reasonably necessary. Nor is it clear that HP Inc. cannot reasonably provide any necessary background without reference to materials the parties deem confidential under the protective order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion 1. For Issuance Of A Letter Rogatory To The High Court Of Hong Kong [Unopposed]; And 2. For An Order Modifying Protective Order To Permit Submission To Hong Kong Court [Opposed] **Redacted**
